PROOF OF SERVICE FILED BY PLAINTIFF INGRAM, BARRY G.

LAW AND MOTION 302, RULING: THE COURT ADOPTED THE TENTATIVE RULING ON DEFENDANTS JAMES ADAMS, SAMSON SABA, RICK FORSTER, AND STONESTOWN SHOPPING CENTER, L.P.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR, IN THE ALTERNATIVE, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION, WHICH WAS NOT CONTESTED. THE COURT GRANTS DEFENDANT'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. THE DECLARATION OF SAMSON SABA ESTABLISHES THAT DEFENDANT'S CONDUCT WAS NOT MOTIVATED BY RACE. DEFENDANT HAS MAINTAINED ITS INITIAL BURDEN OF PRODUCTION. IN OPPOSITION, PLAINTIFF FAILS TO PRESENT COMPETENT EVIDENCE DEMONSTRATING A TRIABLE ISSUE OF MATERIAL FACT REGARDING THAT ISSUE. EVEN IF MR. SABA WAS MISTAKEN REGARDING THE TYPE OF WHEELS ON THE CART, PLAINTIFF FAILS TO SHOW PROOF OF DISCRIMINATORY ANIMUS. THE COURT FURTHER NOTES THAT PLAINTIFF FAILED TO PROVIDE AN OPPOSING SEPARATE STATEMENT AND, SINCE DEFENDANT HAS MET ITS INITIAL BURDEN OF PRODUCTION, THE FAILURE TO PROVIDE AN OPPOSING SEPARATE STATEMENT IS A SEPARATE AND ALTERNATIVE GROUND TO GRANT DEFENDANTS' MOTION UNDER CCP 437C(B)(3). ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER CSR# 5976.

LAW AND MOTION, 302, RULING: THE COURT ADOPTED THE TENTATIVE RULING ON PLAINTIFF BARRY INGRAM'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT, WHICH WAS NOT CONTESTED. THE COURT DENIES PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. PLAINTIFF HAS NOT MET HIS INITIAL BURDEN OF PRODUCTION OF ESTABLISHING THAT THERE IS NO TRIABLE ISSUE OF MATERIAL FACT. FURTHER, PLAINTIFF'S MOTION IS NOT SUPPORTED BY COMPETENT EVIDENCE. THE EVIDENCE IS NOT SUPPORTED BY A DECLARATION. DEFENDANTS' OBJECTIONS TO EVIDENCE ARE SUSTAINED. AS A SEPARATE AND ALTERNATIVE GROUND FOR DENYING THE MOTION, PLAINTIFF HAS FAILED TO PROVIDE A SEPARATE STATEMENT AS REQUIRED BY CCP 437C(B)(1). ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER CSR# 5976.

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Apr-09-2014 Continued To Jul-28-14 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar May-01-2014 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Katelyn Knight, Esq. of Murchison & Cumming LLP, Tel. 415.524.4300, for defendant and moving party;
No other appearance.

RULING: The Court adopted the tentative ruling on DEFENDANTS JAMES ADAMS, SAMSON SABA, RICK FORSTER, and STONESTOWN SHOPPING CENTER, L.P.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication, which was not contested. The Court grants defendant's motion for summary judgment. The declaration of Samson Saba establishes that defendant's conduct was not motivated by race. Defendant has maintained its initial burden of production. In opposition, plaintiff fails to present competent evidence demonstrating a triable issue of material fact regarding that issue. Even if Mr. Saba was mistaken regarding the type of wheels on the cart, plaintiff fails to show proof of discriminatory animus. The Court further notes that plaintiff failed to provide an opposing separate statement and, since defendant has met its initial burden of production, the failure to provide an opposing separate statement is a separate and alternative ground to grant defendants' motion under CCP 437c(b)(3). ORDER SIGNED. Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer CSR# 5976.

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar May-01-2014 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Katelyn Knight, Esq. of Murchison & Cumming LLP, Tel. 415.524.4300, for defendant and moving party;
No other appearance.

RULING: The Court adopted the tentative ruling on PLAINTIFF BARRY INGRAM'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT, which was not contested. The Court denies Plaintiff's motion for summary judgment. Plaintiff has not met his initial burden of production of establishing that there is no triable issue of material fact. Further, Plaintiff's motion is not supported by competent evidence. The evidence is not supported by a declaration. Defendants' objections to evidence are sustained. As a separate and alternative ground for denying the motion, Plaintiff has failed to provide a separate statement as required by CCP 437c(b)(1). ORDER SIGNED. Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer CSR# 5976.

MOTION TO STRIKE 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Mar-26-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: barry ingram, plaintiff in pro per.
katelyn knight, esq., 415.524.4300, for defendants james adams, samson saba, rich forster & stonestown shopping center, l.p.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling: DEFENDANTS JAMES ADAMS, SAMSON SABA, RICK FORSTER, STONESTOWN SHOPPING CENTER, L.P.'S MOTION TO STRIKE 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT is granted as to page 6, beginning at line 5 "'in amount'" through line 11. It is improper for a party to plead the specific amount of punitive damages he or she is requesting in a Complaint. Civil Code Section 3295(e). The motion to strike is denied in all other respects. Order signed in open Court. Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Sherry Sawyer #5976.

DEMURRER TO 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Mar-26-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: barry ingram, plaintiff in pro per.
katelyn knight, esq., 415.524.4300, for defendants james adams, samson saba, rich forster & stonestown shopping center, l.p.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling: DEFENDANTS JAMES ADAMS, SAMSON SABA, RICK FORSTER, STONESTOWN SHOPPING CENTER, L.P.'S DEMURRER TO 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT is overruled. Construed in the light most favorable to the Plaintiff, the Third Amended Complaint alleges sufficient facts to state a cause of action under Civ. Code 51. Order signed in open Court. Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Sherry Sawyer #5976.
